title: Keyboard navigation and skip links

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Keyboard navigation and skip links; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Keyboard navigation and skip links
sources.claim: A skip link is the first practical keyboard shortcut to the primary content target and becomes visible when focused.

sources.locator: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide; living guide accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Keyboard navigation and skip links
sources.claim: A skip link is the first practical keyboard shortcut to the primary content target and becomes visible when focused.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A skip link is the first practical keyboard shortcut to the primary content target and becomes visible when focused.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Pressing Tab first reveals Skip to main content and activating it moves navigation past a long site menu. Your goal is to implement and verify a functional skip link and logical keyboard traversal order. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Sections and landmarks; pf tools files; Meaningful links. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A skip link is the first practical keyboard shortcut to the primary content target and becomes visible when focused. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Its target must exist and receive usable focus or scroll behavior, and it does not replace sensible landmarks and heading order. This draft therefore rejects: Landmarks make skip links unnecessary for every keyboard user.

includes: Preserve source order and provide bypass mechanisms for repeated navigation.
Implement and verify a functional skip link and logical keyboard traversal order.
excludes: Its target must exist and receive usable focus or scroll behavior, and it does not replace sensible landmarks and heading order.

outcomes: Implement and verify a functional skip link and logical keyboard traversal order.

examples.title: Keyboard navigation and skip links: concrete case
examples.situation: Pressing Tab first reveals Skip to main content and activating it moves navigation past a long site menu.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: Pressing Tab first reveals Skip to main content and activating it moves navigation past a long site menu.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Provide a visible-on-focus skip link to the main content target alongside correct landmarks and heading structure.
examples.result: The result follows because Provide a visible-on-focus skip link to the main content target alongside correct landmarks and heading structure.
